hero.sub: AnimeCaos searches, extracts and delivers links straight from the source. All in a clean, fast player with download support. Also integrates with AniList!

features.title: Everything you need. Nothing you don't.
features.sub: Built for people who actually live the anime life.

items.title: Smart Hub
items.desc: Unified search across multiple Brazilian sources in a single query.

items.title: Clean Player
items.desc: Powered by mpv — lightweight, ad-free, no popups, no slowdowns.

items.title: AniList Tracker
items.desc: Sync your progress, mark watched episodes and update your list directly from the app.

items.title: Auto-Play
items.desc: Detects the end of an episode and automatically plays the next one. Watch as many as you want.

items.title: Offline Download
items.desc: Download any episode in the background with real-time progress via yt-dlp.

items.title: Standalone
items.desc: A single .exe with everything bundled. Install, open, watch.

download.title: Ready to start?
download.linux_sub: Ubuntu, Fedora, Arch and any Flatpak distro.
download.cta_sub: Available for Windows, macOS and Linux.

about.title: Why does AnimeCaos exist?
about.p1: Anyone who watches anime knows how it goes: four tabs open, ten ads closed, a player that won't load, an episode that disappears mid-season.
about.p2: AnimeCaos came out of that. It automates the navigation, extracts links directly from the players, and puts everything into an interface that stays out of the way. No server, no account, no subscription.
about.p3: It's open source, built in Python, and ends up being a decent place to learn web automation, scraping, and fuzzy search in practice. Pull requests are welcome.
about.open_desc: The repository is public on GitHub. Anyone can read the code, open an issue, send a PR, or fork the project and take it wherever they want.

howto.title: How to Use
howto.sub: Four steps. Five minutes. Anime playing.

steps.title: Download AnimeCaos
steps.desc: Download the .exe and run it. ⚠️ Firefox must be installed on your PC.

steps.title: Search for an Anime
steps.desc: Type the name in the search box. Fuzzy search finds the title even with typos.

steps.title: Select the Episode
steps.desc: Choose the source and episode. The side panel shows cover and synopsis via AniList automatically.

steps.title: Watch or Download
steps.desc: Click Play to open in mpv, or Download to save in the background to ~/Downloads/Animecaos.

contact.title: Get in touch
contact.sub: Bug? Suggestion? Want to contribute? Come along.
